prompt: FIXED foreground subject: the woman and everything she is wearing. In the source image she is on a plain white studio backdrop — remove that backdrop completely and replace it with the new scene below, but treat HER as a locked, unedited layer. Do NOT regenerate, restyle, relight, reshape, or REPOSE her — keep her exact pose, stance, weight, shoulders, arms, hands, face, expression, hair and skin tone identical to the source. Preserve exactly: warm tan skin; long dark-brown centre-parted hair; a sleeveless ivory/cream tiered cotton midi dress in a tiny pink-and-sage ditsy floral print, scooped neckline with a small self-tie bow, shirred waist, lace-trim band on the lower tier; a fine gold chain; low pearl-embellished clear sandals. Generate ONLY a new photographic background and ground plane. SCENE: stage her casually LEANING against a pale ochre limewashed wall — position the wall plane directly behind and beside her so that wherever her shoulder, upper back or arm meet it, the contact looks natural, with a soft contact shadow at that meeting point. Do not move her body to achieve this; build the wall to her. Soft diffused daylight, open shade — the same warm, even, frontal light already on her, no harsh new shadows across her face or body. The dappled shadow of olive leaves falls gently across the wall from an unseen tree overhead. A rustic weathered-wood bench edge enters low at camera-left; one closed, sun-faded timber shutter sits camera-right. CRITICAL GROUNDING: a clearly visible soft-edged contact shadow beneath her sandals so she is planted, never floating. TONAL UNITY: wall, ground and all shadows in the same warm ochre register as the light on her, so subject and scene read as one single exposure. IMPORTANT: this must MATCH a companion shot of the same woman by the same limewashed wall — identical wall colour, leaf-shadow, light and warm grade. FRAMING: vertical portrait ~2:3, full figure, a little ground beneath her sandals and slight headroom; olive-leaf dapple, clean limewash, bench edge low camera-left.
